tooling_result.reason: The existing focused decode, multiseed object field trace, function call extraction, and literal extraction were sufficient to make this split selector path repeatable and auditable.

events.meaning: Reads the request/event source begin pointer or start field.
Reads the paired request/event source end pointer or limit field.
Derives the request/event source span length from the +0x80/+0x88 pair.

request_event_source.bounded_inference: This selector body uses the same request/event +0x80/+0x88 span shape as the authCode producer, but here the span is used to extract or resolve a field named "code" rather than writing child +0x38.

literal_evidence.role: Likely request/event field name passed to the code extraction helper.
First social-login code compared against the extracted code string.
Second social-login code compared against the extracted code string.
Provider error code compared before the final fallback state.

state_mapping.condition: The extracted code exactly matches SOCIAL_CHALLENGE_ERROR or SOCIAL_ACCOUNT_NOT_LINKED.
The extracted code exactly matches SOCIAL_PROVIDER_NOT_SUPPORTED.
The extracted code does not exactly match the recognized social-login error strings in this path.

call_chain.role: Initial request/event span or temporary string setup.
Helper called with the "code" literal; likely resolves the code field from the request/event material.
Builds or normalizes the extracted code string before comparisons.

negative_evidence: The multiseed field trace for 0x14029E5AC..0x14029E7C6 reports no controller child +0x38 write in this selector body.
The observed controller child writes are all [RDI] state writes: 0x16, 0x17, and 0x18.

confirmed: Selector 0x1A6 from child +0x50 +0x238 enters the 0x14029E5AC split body.
The split body reads request/event +0x80/+0x88, derives a source span length, and uses a literal "code" during extraction.
The extracted code is compared against SOCIAL_CHALLENGE_ERROR, SOCIAL_ACCOUNT_NOT_LINKED, and SOCIAL_PROVIDER_NOT_SUPPORTED.
The controller child state is set to 0x16 for the first two recognized social errors, 0x17 for provider-not-supported, and 0x18 for the unmatched fallback.
reversing_result.bounded_inference: The native 947 client treats selector 0x1A6 as a social-login code response handler. It consumes the same incoming request/event span family as the authCode producer, but routes the result into state transitions instead of persisting the continuation string into child +0x38.
reversing_result.opennxt_relevance: For OpenNXT 947 server work, the web/social-login bridge now has a concrete client-side response contract to emulate: the request/event object must expose a code field whose value drives native client states 0x16, 0x17, or 0x18. This is separate from the authCode/OTL path mapped through selector 0xC8.

next_targets: Trace the selector 0x12E target 0x14029E7C6 to name the request/event +0xD0 route and its child +0x60 service consumer.
Trace the shared tail at 0x14029EA2E to identify which state writes immediately trigger UI, retry, or network side effects.
Use the same focused literal/call/field workflow on the compare helper target 0x1407BB5A0 if string equality semantics need to be named more formally.
